samples.target_compile_errors:
error: `Trait(...)` syntax does not support c_variadic parameters
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:71:31
   |
71 |         let af: [&dyn Fn(u32, ...) -> i32; 2] = [f_var, f_var];
   |                               ^^^ help: remove the `...`

error: defining functions with C-variadic arguments is only allowed for free functions with the "C" or "C-unwind" calling convention
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:12:18
   |
12 | fn f_var(x: u32, _: ...) -> i32 {
   |                  ^^^^^^

error[E0106]: missing lifetime specifier
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:57:25
   |
57 |         let pf: fn() -> &i32 = fip;
   |                         ^ expected named lifetime parameter
   |
   = help: this function's return type contains a borrowed value, but there is no value for it to be borrowed from
help: consider using the `'static` lifetime, but this is uncommon unless you're returning a borrowed value from a `const` or a `static`
   |
57 |         let pf: fn() -> &'static i32 = fip;
   |                          +++++++
help: instead, you are more likely to want to return an owned value
   |
57 -         let pf: fn() -> &i32 = fip;
57 +         let pf: fn() -> i32 = fip;
   |

error[E0658]: C-variadic functions are unstable
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:12:1
   |
12 | / fn f_var(x: u32, _: ...) -> i32 {
13 | |     (x + 3) as i32
14 | | }
   | |_^
   |
   = note: see issue #44930 <https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/44930> for more information
   = help: add `#![feature(c_variadic)]` to the crate attributes to enable
   = note: this compiler was built on 2025-09-09; consider upgrading it if it is out of date

error: usage of an `unsafe` block
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:29:12
   |
29 |         if unsafe { *p } != 1 {
   |            ^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   |
   = note: requested on the command line with `-F unsafe-code`

error[E0277]: `main` has invalid return type `i32`
  --> benchmark_results/2026-05-06T18-03-03/gpt-4o-mini/declarations/type_names/sample_001/target.rs:20:14
   |
20 | fn main() -> i32 {
   |              ^^^ `main` can only return types that implement `Termination`
   |
   = help: consider using `()`, or a `Result`
